summary: Plan-approve and every bead's complete will pass honestly with no --override-adr. (1) adr_citations=[ADR-0030,ADR-0035]: ADR-0030 (Accepted; Domain(s)=execution,validation,lifecycle,lint) covers the execution impacted domain; ADR-0035 (Accepted; Domain(s)=workflow,execution,core) covers the workflow impacted domain. Both Accepted, both intersect {workflow,execution}, so neither adr-coverage-missing (checkADRCoverage) nor adr-cite-irrelevant (checkADRCitations, intersect test) fires. Excluding ADR-0034 (Accepted; Domain(s)=workflow) is a deliberate minimal-covering-set choice, not forced: its workflow domain intersects the impacted set so citing it would NOT trip adr-cite-irrelevant either — the plan's reasoning (lines 40-46) is sound. (2) Every changed file maps to a declared+covered domain per .mindspec/docs/domains/*/OWNERSHIP.yaml: workflow owns cmd/** (B1 next.go; B2 release.go NEW + root.go), internal/next/** (B1 select.go; B3 beads.go), internal/doctor/** (B3 beads.go); execution owns internal/executor/** (B2/B4 mindspec_executor.go) and internal/gitutil/** (B4 gitops.go). New cmd/mindspec/release.go -> cmd/** -> workflow, confirmed. No bead complete needs --override-adr. (3) Under spec 100's merged normalize-then-glob divergence gate (normalizeImpactedDomains + attributeDomain in internal/validate/, present in base tree), attributeDomain iterates ONLY candidateDomains={workflow,execution}; a file owned by an undeclared domain would return empty -> hard adr-divergence-unowned. None of spec 101's source files fall under core (internal/state|config|workspace|spec|idvalidate|speclist|phase|recording|redact|version|journal) or context-system (internal/contextpack|tokenize); all are cmd/**, internal/next/**, internal/doctor/** (workflow) or internal/executor/**, internal/gitutil/** (execution). Spec/plan/panel artifacts under .mindspec/docs/** and review/** are skipped via isProcessArtifact, so they cannot trip the gate. (4) No self-block: all four beads touch only owned+declared+covered files. APPROVE.
